steps.title: Apply the simplification from the given condition
Use the source rationale
steps.content: The correct answer is $5$. Dividing each side of the given equation by $3$ yields ${x}^{2}-6x-5=0$. Adding $5$ to each side of this equation yields ${x}^{2}-6x=5$. Therefore, if $3{x}^{2}-18x-15=0$, the value of ${x}^{2}-6x$ is $5$.
